This is a fill-in template — the concierge deliverable for the validation sprint. It is shown pre-filled with a worked example (“Northwind Outfitters”). To produce one for a real prospect: run A11y Beast on one of their client sites, then replace the bracketed fields — [Your Agency Name], the logo slot, the subject URL, date, record ID, the integrity hash, the score/counts, and the findings — with the real scan output. Then Print → Save as PDF and send it. Keep the honesty notice and disclaimer intact — that is the whole point of the product.
[ YOUR AGENCY LOGO ]
Prepared by [Your Agency Name]
Accessibility Evidence Record
Record ALB-20260624-NW01

Accessibility Evidence Record

Automated legal-risk assessment of Northwind Outfitters — home page

Subject
https://www.northwind-outfitters.com
Scan date (UTC)
24 June 2026, 14:08
Record ID
ALB-20260624-NW01
Engine
A11y Beast · axe-core 4.11.2 + 20 custom (116 checks)
Integrity hash (SHA-256)
9f2c1ad7b4e83a6c0d51e2f4a98b7c33d6e0fb12a45c889e7301b6df42a9c5e8
What this is — and what it isn’t. This is a documented automated assessment of accessibility barriers and the legal frameworks they implicate, captured at a fixed date with a tamper-evident content hash. Automated testing detects roughly 30–40% of WCAG 2.1 success criteria; the remainder require manual and assistive-technology review. This record is evidence of effort and a prioritisation tool — not a certification, not a guarantee of compliance with any law, and not legal advice.

01Summary

62
/ 100
Significant exposure
3
Critical
6
Serious
5
Moderate
14
Total findings

An automated-coverage indicator, weighted by severity and per-framework scope. Lower = higher legal exposure. Not a conformance score.

02Legal exposure — one scan, sixteen verdicts

FrameworkJurisdictionRiskWhy it’s implicated
ADA Title IIIUSAHighHighest US web-litigation volume (3,117 federal suits in 2025); detected barriers map to actionable WCAG failures.
California Unruh ActCalifornia, USAHigh$4,000 statutory minimum per offence; e-commerce is the primary target.
EAA / EN 301 549EU (27 states)MedIn force since 28 Jun 2025; conformance assessed against EN 301 549 v3.2.1 (WCAG 2.1 AA basis).
Section 508USA FederalMedProcurement gate for federal vendors; references WCAG 2.0 AA.
UK Equality Act 2010United KingdomMedEHRC enforcement; reasonable-adjustment duty applies to digital services.
AODAOntario, CanadaLowRegulatory standard (WCAG 2.0 AA); enforcement to date is light.
+ 10 further frameworks mapped in the full record (Section 508 state mirrors, ACA, DDA, JIS X 8341-3, RPD Act, Korea, NZ, Israel, EU WAD, ADA Title II).

03Priority findings

CriticalImages missing alt textWCAG 1.1.1
ADA IIIUnruhEAASection 508
img.product-card__img — 23 occurrences
Fix: Add a concise, descriptive alt attribute to each product image (e.g. the product name + key visual detail). Mark purely decorative images alt="".
CriticalForm input without an accessible labelWCAG 3.3.2 / 4.1.2
ADA IIISection 508EAAAODA
input#newsletter-email
Fix: Associate a visible <label for="newsletter-email"> (or an aria-label if visually hidden by design). Placeholder text is not a label.
SeriousText contrast below 4.5:1WCAG 1.4.3
ADA IIIEAAEN 301 549Unruh
.price--muted (#9a9a9a on #ffffff ≈ 2.8:1)
Fix: Darken the text to at least a 4.5:1 ratio against its background (e.g. #595959 or darker on white).
SeriousNon-descriptive link text (“click here” ×8)WCAG 2.4.4
ADA IIIEAA
a.cta — 8 occurrences
Fix: Make link text describe its destination (“View the summer collection”), so it’s meaningful out of context to screen-reader users.
SeriousMissing skip-navigation linkWCAG 2.4.1
ADA IIISection 508
<body> — no skip link before the nav
Fix: Add a “Skip to main content” link as the first focusable element, targeting the main landmark.
ModerateNo <main> landmarkWCAG 1.3.1
ADA IIIAODA
<body>
Fix: Wrap the primary page content in a <main> element so assistive tech can jump straight to it.

Showing 6 of 14 findings. The full record lists every occurrence with its selector, source snippet and fix.

04Methodology & integrity

The subject page was rendered in a real headless Chromium browser (so JavaScript-built content is evaluated as users experience it), then assessed with axe-core 4.11.2 (96 rules) plus 20 custom checks axe-core doesn’t cover — 116 checks in total. Each finding is mapped to the 16 legal frameworks whose applicable success criteria it implicates, using a fixed rule set (not AI inference, so the legal mapping cannot hallucinate). The engine is publicly benchmarked against axe-core, Pa11y and Lighthouse.

Tamper-evidence. This record’s contents are summarised by the SHA-256 hash above; any later alteration changes the hash, so the document is verifiable as the original. Re-scanning the same site produces a dated successor record, and the difference between two records is the auditable proof of remediation over time — the part a one-off scan cannot give you.

This Accessibility Evidence Record reflects automated testing only and is provided “as is.” Automated tools detect roughly 30–40% of WCAG 2.1 success criteria; full conformance requires manual and assistive-technology evaluation. Nothing here is a certification of compliance, a guarantee against litigation, or legal advice. For a conformance determination, pair this record with a manual audit by a qualified evaluator.
Prepared by [Your Agency Name] Assessment engine: A11y Beast
